# Thylakoid structure in chloroplasts

## Short Teaching Summary
Thylakoid structure in chloroplasts: In eukaryotes (like plants), thylakoids are found inside the chloroplast and appear as stacks or piles of coins called a granum, interconnected by a lamella. Map connection: this idea links by example_of, requires to nearby concepts. Pause point: say the main idea in one short phrase before moving on.
